content.claim: More than 1,200 of 3,155 Hungarian settlements (38%) had no public educational institutions by 2024, creating territorial exclusion from primary education access.
content.context: Rural primary school closures in Hungary would disproportionately harm low-income and Roma students through increased dropout risk and long-term earnings loss, unless accompanied by carefully designed consolidation (multi-grade organization, not exodus-inducing hub schools) and genuine transport/resource equity safeguards.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
